hero.question: Los Angeles Dodgers vs. Washington Nationals
hero.prob_take:
At 6.5%, the market is pricing the Nationals as dead money, implying the Dodgers win roughly 14 times out of 15. That's a massive confidence gap that typically only exists when one team is mathematically eliminated or facing a catastrophic injury.

The Nationals are a real MLB team playing a real game, which means variance alone should keep this floor somewhere north of 3%, so there's edge if you believe in reversion or any late breaking news (roster, weather, bullpen availability) that moves the needle.

daily_take.headline: Dodgers Collapse to 6.5% Is a Gift, Not a Rout
daily_take.deck: The Dodgers just tanked 58 points in 24 hours on what amounts to normal baseball variance. The market panicked on recent losses and overweighted recency bias. At 6.5% against the Nationals, you're getting value on a team with superior talent, deeper pockets, and playoff experience. Sharp money should lean in here.

sidebar.headline: Iran ground op odds jumped 9.5 points. Escalation fears are real, but the market may be pricing in certainty where only possibility exists.
sidebar.label: Israel Iran Op: 22.5%

sidebar.headline: Lakers down 12 points to 52.5%. Mavericks are good, but Laker depth is being discounted hard in a single-game market prone to swing trades.
sidebar.label: Lakers vs Mavericks: 52.5%

sidebar.headline: Hormuz traffic recovery at 13.5% with massive volume. That mega-low probability on a $2.9M bet suggests sophisticated money sees escalation risk lasting longer than April.
sidebar.label: Strait of Hormuz Normal: 13.5%
